title: Memory lineage is first-class repo knowledge governance
summary: Kage now has first class memory supersession and lineage. Use kage supersede packet <old id replacement <new id reason <why when a newer packet replaces old repo knowledge; the old packet becomes superseded, recall ignor
body: Kage now has first-class memory supersession and lineage. Use kage supersede --packet <old-id> --replacement <new-id> --reason <why> when a newer packet replaces old repo knowledge; the old packet becomes superseded, recall ignores it, bidirectional lineage edges are written, and kage lineage plus the viewer Memory page show current replacements and orphaned retired packets.

context.fact: Kage now has first-class memory supersession and lineage.
